steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures at the blank
steps.content: Left side: the end of a relative clause describing the alliance (“that dominated northern European trade between the 13th and 17th centuries”). Right side: a noun phrase with appositive information (“the Hanseatic League, a loose confederation...”).

steps.title: Apply the colon rule for identifying phrases
steps.content:
A colon introduces an identifying or clarifying phrase that elaborates on what was just mentioned.

It correctly marks “the Hanseatic League” as the specific name of the alliance being described.

The period awkwardly separates the identification from what it identifies, the semicolon incorrectly requires two independent clauses when only a noun phrase follows, and the lack of punctuation violates basic sentence structure.

That confirms D is correct.
